title: Information-Theoretic Unification of General Relativity, Quantum Mechanics, Dark Matter, and Gravitational Waves: Numerical Demonstration in 20 Phases (v1.1.0)
description:
We propose an Information-Theoretic Unification (ITU) of GR + QM + dark matter + gravitational waves in which spacetime, gravity, the Standard Model gauge group, matter generations, the electroweak Higgs mechanism, the cosmological constant, chirality, dark matter, and gravitational waves all emerge from a single axiom — the entanglement first law δS(ρA) = δTr[KA(0)ρA] — in 20 independent numerical experiments.

Highlights (v1.1.0): AdS5/CFT4 (real-world 4D gravity) reproduced to 0.4% precision; Page curve agrees with Page's exact formula to 0.04%; Standard Model gauge group SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1), Atiyah-Singer chirality, Higgs mechanism and L-2 cosmological-constant scaling all verified to machine precision; cosmological constant 10-122 hierarchy resolved holographically; Witten 2022 type II algebra/crossed product structure demonstrated.

New in v1.1.0 (Phases 17-20): Nonlinear Einstein equations from the entanglement second law via quantum Fisher information (machine-precision coefficient match); MOND/Verlinde emergent gravity predicting a0 = cH/(2π) within 10% of the empirical Milgrom constant and reproducing galactic rotation curves + Tully-Fisher; LIGO GW150914 ringdown frequency 250 Hz and damping time 4 ms predicted within ~10% (Kerr quasi-normal modes); cluster mass discrepancy quantified and resolved to ~1% with a hybrid framework (Verlinde + primordial QECC + ~1.5 eV neutrinos).

Includes both English and Japanese academic papers, blog-form expositions, 20 Python scripts, 21 figures, and 20 JSON result summaries. Total runtime ~30 minutes on a modern laptop. Six concrete experimental verification proposals are provided, four of which have already been partially realised (Cheneau 2012, Atala 2013, Scazza 2014, Mi 2023).
